title: SiteFlow vs NYGGS | Construction Software Comparison
metaTitle: SiteFlow vs NYGGS | Construction Software Comparison
metaDescription: Compare SiteFlow vs NYGGS and get to know why SiteFlow is better construction management ERP software.
author: SiteFlow Operations Team
body:
Construction ERP Comparison

SiteFlow vs NYGGS

Which fits your site best?

NYGGS is a cloud-based construction ERP covering project management, procurement, finance, payroll, fleet, and GST compliance. SiteFlow approaches the problem from a field-first perspective — linking BOQ, budgeting, labour, materials, and billing in one chain so that every site activity immediately reflects project cost and profitability.

Platform Overview

Two Different Approaches to Construction Software

Both SiteFlow and NYGGS are construction management platforms — but they are built around different philosophies. Understanding their priorities helps you choose the right fit for your team.

── SiteFlow CARD — cascade class removed ──
● SiteFlow

What is SiteFlow

SiteFlow is a construction management software built specifically around BOQ-driven execution, cost control, and live site visibility. It connects scope definition, installed quantities, labour attendance, material usage, subcontractor billing, and project finances into one continuous workflow.

SiteFlow is best suited for construction companies that want:
  • ✓BOQ-linked budgeting, progress tracking, and billing
  • ✓Real-time capture of labour, material, and site activity
  • ✓Integrated subcontractor work orders and running bills
  • ✓Budget vs actual dashboards aligned with execution
  • ✓Native integration with commonly used accounting systems
Why construction teams choose SiteFlow:
  • ✓Designed primarily for execution-centric control rather than enterprise documentation governance
  • ✓Works best for contractors managing multiple active projects with BOQ-based scope
  • ✓Focuses on live commercial clarity instead of post-execution reporting

Pricing: SiteFlow follows a project-friendly software pricing model designed for construction teams of different sizes.

── NYGGS CARD — cascade class removed ──
NYGGS SVG mock — green colours are NYGGS brand, intentionally kept
● NYGGS

What is NYGGS

NYGGS is a cloud-based construction ERP software that connects project planning and scheduling, materials management, procurement, finance and accounting, HR and payroll, fleet and fuel management, equipment tracking, subcontractor management, and GST compliance into one integrated platform for construction, infrastructure, road, and mining companies across India.

NYGGS is typically suited for teams that need:
  • ✓Project planning and scheduling with planned versus actual deviation reporting
  • ✓Materials management covering inventory tracking, MRN generation, and automated reordering
  • ✓Fleet and fuel management with AIS 140-compliant GPS tracking
  • ✓Finance and accounting with GST compliance and automated tax reporting
  • ✓HR and payroll management across large multi-site workforces
Important considerations:
  • ⚠Serves multiple industries beyond construction — manufacturing, logistics, mining, healthcare — which can dilute construction-specific depth
  • ⚠Pricing starts at Rs 5,000 per user per month — significantly higher than SiteFlow’s per-user cost
  • ⚠Execution-level cost visibility often depends on data consolidation across departments rather than flowing directly from BOQ-level site activity

Pricing: Starts at Rs 5,000 per user per month. Contact NYGGS directly for enterprise pricing details based on modules and deployment scale.

Script removed — only purpose was scroll cascade
Header — cascade classes removed
Key Differentiators

Key Differences Between SiteFlow & NYGGS That Impact Daily Project Control

Four critical areas where SiteFlow and NYGGS take fundamentally different approaches — each with real impact on your daily execution.

LEFT PANEL — cascade classes removed
Execution vs Enterprise Coverage
Comparison
✓ SiteFlow

Places clear focus on linking site activity with financial impact. Labour entries, procurement approvals, and progress updates feed directly into cost tracking so financial dashboards reflect real activity as it happens. Execution and commercial data move together from the start, reducing heavy reconciliation.

● NYGGS

Provides wide ERP functionality across administrative, financial, and operational areas. Helps businesses consolidate multiple departments — but execution and cost data often flow through department-level consolidation before the full picture becomes visible to leadership.

✓ SiteFlow

Concentrates on financial discipline during day-to-day operations. Approvals and workflows are aligned with budget limits before expenses are committed, keeping control at the execution stage rather than only at the reporting stage. Overruns are visible before they compound.

● NYGGS

Built around planned versus actual comparisons and consolidated reporting across multiple projects. Management gets a broad financial view once data from different functions comes together — which is powerful for oversight but happens after expenses are committed.

✓ SiteFlow

Focuses workforce control at the point of attendance. Face recognition combined with GPS confirmation verifies that the specific worker being paid was physically present at the correct site at check-in — addressing payroll fraud at entry rather than through post-cycle audit.

● NYGGS

Includes a proprietary AIS 140-compliant GPS device for real-time fleet and vehicle tracking — particularly suited for infrastructure and road contractors where machinery movement, fuel consumption, and equipment utilization are central operational concerns.

✓ SiteFlow

Built exclusively for construction. BOQ-linked procurement, GRN-based bill matching, retention tracking, RA billing, and geo-tagged attendance are construction-specific requirements that SiteFlow treats as core rather than configurable add-ons.

● NYGGS

Serves multiple industries beyond construction including manufacturing, logistics, mining, healthcare, and banking. Construction is one of several verticals — which means the platform is configurable but may not carry the same construction-specific workflow depth.

Make Your Decision

Which Platform Fits Your Workflow?

The right choice depends on whether your priority is construction-specific execution control or enterprise-wide ERP coverage across multiple departments and industries.

NYGGS card — cascade classes removed; green brand kept
NYGGS
May suit you if

Choose NYGGS if your operations span multiple departments or industries where broad ERP integration and fleet tracking are the primary management challenge.

  • ✓Your operations span road, highway, or infrastructure contracting where AIS 140-compliant fleet and vehicle GPS tracking is a core requirement
  • ✓GST compliance and NHAI-aligned billing management are central to your financial workflows
  • ✓Fleet movement, fuel consumption, and equipment utilization are as critical as project tracking
  • ✓You need multi-department ERP coverage — finance, HR, payroll, procurement, and project management in one platform
  • ✓Your business spans multiple industries beyond construction, such as mining, logistics, or manufacturing
SiteFlow card — cascade classes removed; dot class updated
SiteFlow
May suit you if

Choose SiteFlow if your business needs execution-driven control, BOQ-based tracking, real-time cost visibility, and fast site adoption specifically for construction.

  • ✓BOQ-driven execution control — every procurement, billing, and cost entry connects back to scope items in real time
  • ✓Real-time cost visibility without waiting for end-of-month departmental consolidation
  • ✓Face recognition and GPS attendance — payroll fraud addressed at entry, not through post-cycle audit
  • ✓Preventive budget controls — approval workflows check limits before expenses are committed
  • ✓Construction-specific depth — BOQ procurement, GRN bill matching, retention tracking, and RA billing as core features
  • ✓Zero double-entry accounting — native Tally and Zoho Books integration for contractors who do not need a full ERP
  • ✓Fast site adoption — designed for engineers and supervisors to go live in under a day without heavy configuration
════ PART B: FAQ ════
cascade class removed
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about SiteFlow vs NYGGS — answered directly.

LEFT COLUMN

SiteFlow is built exclusively for construction site execution, connecting BOQ, labour, materials, procurement, and billing into one execution-driven workflow. NYGGS is a broader ERP platform covering construction, infrastructure, mining, and other industries with modules for fleet, fuel, HR, and GST compliance. SiteFlow gives deeper construction-specific control while NYGGS offers wider enterprise coverage.

SiteFlow works as a strong alternative to NYGGS for construction companies that want BOQ-driven execution control, real-time cost visibility, and fast site adoption. Contractors, builders, EPC firms, and interior companies typically choose SiteFlow because every module is designed specifically for how construction projects are planned, executed, and financially controlled — without the broader ERP complexity.

SiteFlow tracks site progress more directly by tying updates to BOQ items and installed quantities. Site engineers update progress from mobile, and the system reflects those updates immediately in cost views and billing readiness. NYGGS tracks planned vs actual deviations through its ERP reporting framework, which gives broad financial comparisons but often requires data consolidation before management sees execution-level insights.

NYGGS suits road, highway, and infrastructure contractors well because of its proprietary AIS 140-compliant GPS device for fleet and vehicle tracking, its GST compliance and NHAI-aligned billing management, and its multi-department ERP coverage. For contractors where fleet movement, fuel consumption, and equipment utilization are as critical as project tracking, NYGGS offers purpose-built capabilities in those areas.

RIGHT COLUMN

SiteFlow verifies labour at the point of attendance using face recognition combined with GPS confirmation, addressing payroll fraud before it enters the payroll cycle. It tracks daily productivity and generates automated salary calculations from verified attendance. NYGGS provides attendance tracking and a full payroll module as part of its HR system, which covers broader workforce administration across large multi-site operations.

SiteFlow treats the BOQ as the operational backbone of every project. Budget exposure, procurement, billing, and progress tracking all connect back to BOQ items — so cost position is always visible relative to scope. NYGGS has a budget module with planned vs actual comparison, but its cost tracking operates within an ERP framework where execution data feeds in from multiple departments rather than flowing directly from BOQ-level site activity.

Teams often use SiteFlow without a separate ERP for daily project tracking, cost visibility, billing readiness, and reporting. For accounting, SiteFlow integrates natively with Tally and Zoho Books, eliminating double entry. NYGGS is designed as a complete ERP, so it includes finance and accounting internally rather than requiring external integrations.

Choose SiteFlow if your business needs execution-driven control, BOQ-based tracking, real-time cost visibility, and fast site adoption specifically for construction. Choose NYGGS if your operations span multiple departments beyond construction — including fleet, fuel, HR, and GST compliance — or if you are in infrastructure, road, or mining where NYGGS’s proprietary GPS and NHAI billing alignment provide direct operational value.
